$comment: Full 9-field catalog for Server-Side Request Forgery (previously a null code-review catalog).

patterns.what_to_look_for: Server fetches a URL/host taken from request input without an allow-list (webhooks, link preview, import-by-URL, avatar fetch).
source_indicators: http client called with a request-derived URL
blackbox_indicators: the server makes a request to an attacker-controlled collaborator host
evidence_required: an out-of-band callback to a controlled host (benign)
false_positive_checks: only a fixed base URL with a constrained path suffix
patterns.suggested_validation_task: Point the URL param at a controlled out-of-band collaborator and confirm the server-side request (benign, no internal target).
patterns.reporting_guidance: Distinguish full SSRF from blind SSRF; note metadata-endpoint and internal-service reach.

patterns.what_to_look_for: Allow-list bypass via redirect-following, DNS rebinding, or parser confusion (userinfo@, octal/hex IPs).
source_indicators: URL validated once but client follows redirects
host parsed differently than the fetcher uses it
blackbox_indicators: a redirect to an internal host is followed
evidence_required: the validation gap (parse vs fetch mismatch or redirect-follow)
patterns.suggested_validation_task: Serve a redirect from an allowed host to a controlled host; confirm it is followed.
patterns.reporting_guidance: Emphasize that one-time validation is insufficient when redirects are followed.

patterns.what_to_look_for: Cloud metadata / internal-service reach (169.254.169.254, link-local, .internal) not blocked.
blackbox_indicators: metadata endpoint content returned or timing-distinguishable
false_positive_checks: egress firewall blocks the range regardless of app code
patterns.suggested_validation_task: Attempt a benign reach to a known internal/link-local target (only with explicit scope + active-poc gate).
patterns.reporting_guidance: Critical when cloud metadata credentials are reachable; gate any live proof behind active-poc.
